step_by_step_thinking:
Posterior interproximal caries can best be diagnosed via bite-wing radiographs, specifically those employing the bisecting angle technique, ensuring optimal visualization of the occlusal contacts and detailed surface anatomy.
\boxed{A} Based on the evidence emphasizing the effectiveness and accuracy of posterior bitewing radiographs for detecting interproximal caries, particularly with the bisecting angle technique enhancing visibility and detail, the correct approach aligns closely with option A.
